channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Before Making a Deal, Trump Demanded Iran’s Surrender. He Got a Surprise.
item.description: While the Iranians suffered substantial losses in the war, they emerged from a confrontation with the world’s most powerful military having proved they can use economic chaos as a weapon.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump at a Group of 7 summit in France on Wednesday. The president has argued he has the upper hand with an agreement that leaves negotiations over Iran’s nuclear program for a later date.

item.title: Trump Upends G7 Summit With Statements on Iran Deal and Ukraine
item.description: At the Group of 7 gathering in France, President Trump’s oscillations on his Iran deal and Ukraine left European leaders racing to catch up.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler
item.media:description: President Trump at the G7 summit in Évian-les-Bains, France, on Wednesday.

item.title: U.S.-Iran Agreement Includes Strait of Hormuz, Lebanon and More: See Full Text of the Deal
item.description: New York Times reporters annotated the agreement, which a senior official disclosed on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament

item.title: Trump Defends Deal to End the War With Iran as Details Emerge
item.description: President Trump lashed out at critics who say the agreement achieves less than the one President Barack Obama signed in 2015, and he threatened to bomb Iran again if it violated the deal.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green, Zolan Kanno-Youngs, Farnaz Fassihi and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump and other U.S. officials at a news conference amid the Group of 7 summit in Évian-les-Bains, France, on Wednesday.

item.title: Trump Feuds With Thune and G.O.P., Stoking Election-Year Rift
item.description: The president upended the majority leader’s plans by yanking his intelligence nominee from a confirmation hearing and insisting on an end to the filibuster.
item.dc:creator: Carl Hulse
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Office of the Director of National Intelligence
item.media:description: Senator John Thune, Republican of South Dakota and the majority leader, has shown more willingness in recent days to push back against President Trump no matter the fallout.

item.title: Bill Pulte, a Bulldog for Trump, Prepares to Take Reins at Spy Agency
item.description: Democrats and some Republicans excoriated President Trump’s pick for a top national security job. The president pushed ahead anyway.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Dustin Volz
item.category: Office of the Director of National Intelligence
item.media:description: Bill Pulte has used his current post as a top federal housing official to help with President Trump’s campaign of retribution against his perceived enemies.

item.title: Trump Administration to Pay $765 Million to Cancel 4 More Wind Projects
item.description: It’s the third such deal the Interior Department has struck to pay firms to abandon plans for offshore turbines, spending roughly $2.5 billion to get companies to abandon their wind projects.
item.dc:creator: Brad Plumer
item.media:description: The Trump administration has now struck deals energy developers totaling more than $2.5 billion to cancel offshore wind leases.

item.title: Warsh Makes His Case With Jargon, and a Penchant for Detail
item.description: The Federal Reserve’s new chairman held a press conference where he bantered with reporters and laid out a vision for change at the central bank.
item.dc:creator: Sydney Ember
item.media:credit: Anna Rose Layden for The New York Times
item.media:description: Kevin M. Warsh, the Federal Reserve chairman, after the central bank’s meeting in Washington on Wednesday.

item.title: Fed Meeting Live Updates: Kevin Warsh to Address U.S. Economy as Fed Holds Interest Rates Steady
item.description: Officials at the Federal Reserve were split between no cuts this year and one or more rate increases as they braced for higher inflation, according to a new set of projections.
item.dc:creator: Colby Smith

item.title: Billionaire Tax Officially Has Enough Signatures for California Ballot
item.description: A proposed wealth tax on billionaires is headed to the ballot unless Gov. Gavin Newsom and other opponents reach a deal with backers to remove it.
item.dc:creator: Soumya Karlamangla
item.category: Service Employees International Union-United Healthcare Workers West
item.media:credit: Mike Kai Chen for The New York Times
item.media:description: A proposed wealth tax on billionaires has qualified for the November ballot.

item.title: New Plan Scales Back C.D.C.’s Work on Diseases Abroad
item.description: The State Department is taking over much of the control of global health initiatives, for which critics say the department does not have the expertise.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
President's Emergency Plan for AIDS Relief
item.media:description: Demonstrators in Washington in 2025 protest the withholding of funding from the President’s Emergency Plan for AIDS Relief, PEPFAR.

item.title: Together We Stand: A Knicks Celebration and a Pause for Communal Joy
item.description: The Knicks’ parade through the Canyon of Heroes on Thursday is expected to attract legions of fans celebrating the team’s first championship in 53 years.
item.dc:creator: Jonah E. Bromwich, Dana Rubinstein and Santul Nerkar
item.media:credit: José A. Alvarado Jr. for The New York Times
item.media:description: The crowded scene at a watch party in Greenwich Village on Saturday is expected to be replicated many times over at the Canyon of Heroes parade on Thursday.

item.title: What to Know if You’re Going to the Knicks Parade
item.description: New Yorkers will descend on Lower Manhattan on Thursday morning to celebrate the Knicks. Here’s what you should know if you are one of them.
item.dc:creator: Ed Shanahan and Sarah Maslin Nir
item.media:credit: Mimi d’Autremont for The New York Times
item.media:description: The parade starts at 10 a.m., but you’ll want to get there at least two hours earlier.

item.title: Knicks Accept a White House Invitation to Celebrate Title, Owner Says
item.description: James Dolan said he was “very proud” to bring his championship team to Washington. It would be the first N.B.A. team to visit the White House with Trump as president.
item.dc:creator: Santul Nerkar and Tyler Pager
item.media:description: James Dolan, the owner of the Knicks, center, celebrated the team’s championship win on Saturday.

item.title: Why Waymo’s Driverless Taxis Won’t Be on Your Streets Anytime Soon
item.description: The Silicon Valley company’s ambitions to roll out autonomous cars nationwide have hit political roadblocks in some of the country’s biggest markets.
item.dc:creator: David McCabe
item.media:credit: Manuel Orbegozo for The New York Times

item.title: A New Era of Super-Hybrids to Ease Range Anxiety
item.description: Extended-range electric vehicles promise to reduce costs and appeal to America’s appetite for adventure. And they’re coming to showrooms soon.
item.dc:creator: Tom Voelk
item.media:description: The technology that powers extended-range electric vehicles, like the 2026 Jeep Grand Wagoneer, solves the towing issues common among regular electric vehicles.

item.title: Why the By-Election in Makerfield Will Have Giant Consequences for the UK
item.description: Here is what to know about the by-election on Thursday in Makerfield, the outcome of which could lead to Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s ouster.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:description: Andy Burnham, the mayor of Greater Manchester, giving a speech in Wigan, England, on Saturday.

item.title: In Southeast Asia, Switching to Solar Offers Relief from Energy Crisis Caused by Iran War
item.description: Homeowners and businesses in the region, which relies heavily on imported oil, are installing rooftop solar panels to reduce the sting of soaring energy costs.
item.dc:creator: Zunaira Saieed and Aie Balagtas See
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Jes Aznar for The New York Times
item.media:description: Setting up a solar power system at a home in Quezon City, Philippines, this month.

item.title: A Tournament on Native Land Makes Millions. A Tribe Wants a Bigger Cut.
item.description: The Shinnecock Hills Golf Club on Long Island earns millions when it hosts the U.S. Open. But the nearby Indian tribe has for years only received a fraction of that.
item.dc:creator: Corey Kilgannon
item.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
item.media:description: Lisa Goree, the chairwoman of the Shinnecock Indian Nation, why says the golf course hosting the U.S. Open this week on Long Island was built on their land.

item.title: We Want to Hear from Indigenous People Across New York.
item.description: The New York Times is examining the relationship between New York State officials and Native Americans.
item.dc:creator: Jay Root and Bianca Pallaro
item.media:credit: Cindy Schultz for The New York Times
item.media:description: Harry Wallace, the chief of the Unkechaug Indian Nation, wants state officials to remove artwork from the Capitol depicting Indigenous peoples.

item.title: Tornadoes Reported in Wisconsin and Illinois as Violent Storms Sweep the Midwest
item.description: The National Weather Service issued several of its highest-level tornado alerts on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Nazaneen Ghaffar and Amy Graff

item.title: Tropical Storm Arthur Forecast: How Much Rain Will Fall?
item.description: Heavy rain and flash flooding are expected across the region through Friday.
item.dc:creator: Judson Jones
item.media:description: Floodwaters rose in Galveston, Texas, on Wednesday.

item.title: This Is Not a Grand Bargain With Iran. Good.
item.description: The world doesn’t need a nuclear deal with Tehran.
item.dc:creator: Michael Singh
item.category: Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Illustration by Sam Whitney/The New York Times

item.title: In Britain, the Far Right Is Setting the Agenda
item.description: Its opponents urgently need to demonstrate that they can do more than throw up their hands in horror.
item.dc:creator: William Davies

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel Feels ‘Hormuzled’ by Trump’s New Deal With Iran
item.description: Although the agreement finally reopens the Strait of Hormuz, “we threw in a minimum of $300 billion,” Kimmel said. “Right now, Melania’s wondering, ‘How do I get a deal like that?’”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: 18-Year-Old Dies in Fall From Horse Carriage in Central Park
item.description: After the driver stepped out to take a photo of the passengers, the horse bolted, and a tourist from India fell from the driverless carriage. “We were yelling, ‘Help me, help me!’” said his father.
item.dc:creator: Andy Newman, Miles G. Cohen and Chelsia Rose Marcius
item.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
item.media:description: The accident was the latest in a series of mishaps involving horse carriages.
